topic: Introduction to common data formats (CSV, TSV, and JSON) used for importing external datasets into creative coding projects.

concepts.name: Tabular Data (CSV/TSV)
concepts.explanation: Data organized into rows and columns, similar to an Excel or Google Sheets spreadsheet. It uses delimiters like commas (CSV) or tabs (TSV) to separate values.
concepts.why_it_matters: It is a simple, human-readable way to store structured information that can be easily mapped to 2D arrays in code.

concepts.name: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation)
concepts.explanation: A hierarchical data format that is a subset of JavaScript objects. It uses key-value pairs (e.g., "Name": "London") and can be nested with arrays and other objects.
concepts.why_it_matters: It is the standard for web communication and allows for much more complex, nested data structures than simple tables.

concepts.name: Delimiters
concepts.explanation: The characters (like commas or tabs) used to separate individual pieces of data within a single line of text.
concepts.why_it_matters: Understanding these is crucial for correctly parsing raw text files into usable programming structures.

code_examples.demonstrates: The raw text representation of a CSV file where values are separated by commas.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting the comma or using a different character will prevent the data from being parsed correctly.

code_examples.demonstrates: A JSON array containing multiple objects, each with key-value pairs.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to put keys in double quotes or using a function instead of a string/number.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Trying to include a function inside a JSON file and having it fail.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: JSON is data-only; it cannot store executable code/functions.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Store the function as a string and handle the logic within your JavaScript code.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Data not loading or showing as 'undefined'.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Missing quotes around keys in a JSON file.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Ensure all keys are enclosed in double quotes (e.g., "Name" instead of Name).

key_moments.description: The instructor explains that social media sites don't send entire graphics; they send structured data (objects) and reference the assets.
The instructor notes that JSON doesn't have to be 'pretty-printed' with indentation; it can be minified on one line.

student_questions: What is the difference between CSV and JSON?
Why are my data files just text?
How do I use a spreadsheet in my code?
Can I put functions inside a JSON file?
Why do the keys in my JSON need quotes?
How does a CSV file look when it's not in Excel?
What is the difference between a comma and a tab in data files?
How do I access specific info from a big list of data?
Is JSON just a JavaScript object?
module: Module 4 – Poetry of The World, Oceans of Data
